Plaintiffs' Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Part 2 Of 2)
Off Calendar Jun-08-2017 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Jun-08-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties George Vorpahl and Patricia Vorpahl
Laura Przetak of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF
No objection made and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The trial date is September 25, 2017 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is October 6, 2017. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is September 20, 2017. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than August 11, 2017. Responses to the demand are due no later than August 25, 2017. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by September 18, 2017 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on September 21, 2017. Fact discovery cut-off date is September 8, 2017. Expert discovery cut-off date is September 22, 2017. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than July 7, 2017. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than June 22, 2017 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than June 22, 2017 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on July 25, 2017 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to CRC, Rule 3.1380 and San Francisco Local Rules of Court, Rules 5.0 and 20.2 on September 25, 2017 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rules 5.0 and 20.2 and provide a confidential settlement statement by September 21, 2017 at 2:00p.m. J-M Manufacturing filed a conditional opposition that the Court did not consider for failure to provide a courtesy copy. (Local Rule 2.7(B).) Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter Not Reported (503)
